---
title: "About"
---

## Introduction

**MGnify Biosynthetic Gene Clusters** is a portal for exploring predicted biosynthetic gene clusters (BGCs) across MGnify metagenomic datasets. It standardises the output of several widely used detection tools (antiSMASH, GECCO, SanntiS) so you can quickly compare and prioritise candidate regions without setting up complex software. Non‑technical users can work entirely through the web interface; programmatic users can extend their analysis via the API.

MGnify Biosynthetic Gene Clusters aims to make this valuable dataset widely accessible, promoting advances in natural product science across research, industry, and the broader community. The portal is part of [MGnify](https://www.ebi.ac.uk/metagenomics), which offers automated pipelines for the analysis and archiving of microbiome data. MGnify helps determine the taxonomic diversity and functional and metabolic potential of environmental samples. Users can submit their data for analysis or browse the publicly available datasets within the repository. Additionally, MGnify can analyse datasets from the European Nucleotide Archive (ENA) upon request.

## Dataset overview

The underlying dataset collects BGC predictions from MGnify metagenomic assemblies using antiSMASH [1], GECCO [2] and SanntiS [3]. Outputs are harmonised and stored with links to contigs, assemblies and protein annotations. The open [pipeline](https://github.com/Finn-Lab/BGCdetection_pipeline) describes the processing steps.

## Funding and support

This portal has been developed at EMBL-EBI by the MGnify team as part of the [EUREMAP](https://euremap.eu) project. EUREMAP is a European initiative designed to create an integrated pipeline of marine bioprospecting services to discover novel bioactive natural products from marine organisms. This project connects research infrastructures, industry, and academia strengthening the blue economy.

EUREMAP project is funded by the European Union under grant agreement No. 101131663 (HORIZON-INFRA-2023-DEV-01-04). Views and opinions expressed are however those of the author(s) only and do not necessarily reflect those of the European Union or the Agency. Neither the European Union nor the granting authority can be held responsible for them.

## Contact

MGnify Biosynthetic Gene Clusters is currently in beta and under active development. We value feedback and encourage users to share any issues or suggestions via our [contact form](https://www.ebi.ac.uk/about/contact/support/metagenomics).

## References

[1] Blin, Kai, Simon Shaw, Hannah E. Augustijn, Zachary L. Reitz, Friederike Biermann, Mohammad Alanjary, Artem Fetter et al. "antiSMASH 7.0: new and improved predictions for detection, regulation, chemical structures and visualisation." Nucleic acids research 51, no. W1 (2023): W46-W50.

[2] Carroll, Laura M., Martin Larralde, Jonas Simon Fleck, Ruby Ponnudurai, Alessio Milanese, Elisa Cappio, and Georg Zeller. "Accurate de novo identification of biosynthetic gene clusters with GECCO." BioRxiv (2021): 2021-05.

[3] Sanchez, Santiago, Joel D. Rogers, Alexander B. Rogers, Maaly Nassar, Johanna McEntyre, Martin Welch, Florian Hollfelder, and Robert D. Finn. "Expansion of novel biosynthetic gene clusters from diverse environments using SanntiS." bioRxiv (2023): 2023-05.
